column1: SeAH Wind Teesworks Monopile Manufacturing Facility
column6: South Bank, Teesworks, Redcar and Cleveland
column24: UK Government and Tees Valley Combined Authority
column27: Directly located in Tees Valley. Reported capital investment of £900m and 750 direct jobs. Annual production capacity reported as up to 200 monopiles.

column1: JDR Cable Systems Cambois Subsea Cable Facility
column24: JDR Cable Systems and North East Combined Authority
column27: Direct North East investment in a high-voltage subsea cable manufacturing plant. Reported to create 171 direct jobs. Completion and operating status should be updated when formally confirmed.
Direct Sunderland investment. Reported capital investment of £1bn and 1,000 jobs, with stated annual production capacity of 15.8 GWh.

column2: Gas-fired power station with carbon capture
column27: Reached financial close in December 2024. Construction began from 2025 with operations targeted from 2028. Reported figures include more than 3,000 construction jobs and around 1,000 jobs supported during operations. The 4,000 figure is retained as the overall announced regional jobs headline and must not be added to the component categories. The approximately £4bn construction-contract figure covers NZT Power and the Northern Endurance Partnership jointly, so it is not recorded as standalone project capital investment.

column1: Port of Blyth Battleship Wharf Expansion
column6: Battleship Wharf, Port of Blyth, Northumberland
column8: Port of Blyth; Northumberland County Council; North East Combined Authority; The Crown Estate
column27: Direct regional port infrastructure investment of up to £100m. Planned works include approximately three hectares of reclaimed land, up to 260m of quay extensions, channel and berth deepening, and heavy-duty handling equipment. The source refers to hundreds of potential jobs but does not state a precise verified figure, so jobs fields remain blank.

Direct £150m regional investment in a 230-acre clean-energy development terminal. The source describes capacity to support offshore wind and other clean-energy projects. Up to 12,000 jobs is a wider jobs-supported ambition associated with the development and must not be interpreted as direct operational employment.

Direct Teesside hydrogen project at pre-application stage. The £2bn figure is potential investment rather than committed capital. Project sources describe more than 100 new operational jobs and an estimated £200m–£300m contribution to the local economy; this row records £200m as a conservative lower-bound economic impact estimate. Capacity reflects planned expansion to approximately 1,065 MW thermal.
